commands.repair_reason: A human should require the security-boundary fix and contributor-supplied real behavior proof before merge; ClawSweeper should not take over repair while the external proof gate is unmet.; Needs attention: The diff fixes one ACL blast radius but introduces an unpinned chmod operation on a security-sensitive workspace path.; Review finding: [P1] Avoid path-based chmod on resolved dreams directories — extensions/memor... (sha=4b6f99a7796f2363d54a6018222f9d65997289e4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a memory-core dreaming private-store helper, routes dreaming JSON state writes through it, and adds focused tests for ACL blast radius and symlink-swap behavior under `memory/.dreams`.

Reproducibility: yes. by source and linked live evidence: current `main` writes memory-core dreaming JSON under `memory/.dreams` through `privateFileStore(workspaceDir)`, and the related Linux ACL repro shows that private-store path chmodding the shared workspace parents.
commands.review_followup: A human should require the security-boundary fix and contributor-supplied real behavior proof before merge; ClawSweeper should not take over repair while the external proof gate is unmet.

target.title: fix(memory-core): preserve dreaming private workspace boundary [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Replace workspace-rooted dreaming private JSON writes with a memory-core helper that uses `fs-safe`'s workspace-root `root()` API for read/write containment.
- Keep dreaming artifacts under `memory/.dreams` private without chmodding the user-facing workspace root or `memory/` parent directory.
- Add regression coverage for the ACL blast radius and for a symlink-swap attempt during `memory/.dreams` setup.

Supersedes and replaces #85435. Refs #56263; this targets the memory-core dreaming private-store manifestation described in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56263#issuecomment-4520113754 without claiming to resolve the broader permission-policy issue.

## Why

The previous approach narrowed the private store root to `memory/.dreams`, which fixed the ACL blast radius but lost the original workspace realpath containment boundary. This version avoids both bad choices:

- it no longer calls `privateFileStore(workspaceDir)`, so fs-safe does not chmod the workspace root and `memory/` parent to `0700`;
- it also does not call `privateFileStore(workspace/memory/.dreams)`, so the write path keeps the outer workspace boundary through `root(workspaceDir)`.

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Memory-core dreaming private JSON writes should preserve the shared workspace root and `memory/` permissions while keeping private JSON artifacts under the original workspace boundary.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux checkout, with the targeted memory-core regression run inside Docker using `node:24-bookworm`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
docker run --rm -v "$PWD":/repo -w /repo node:24-bookworm bash -lc 'node --version && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-private-store.test.ts'
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
v24.15.0

RUN  v4.1.7 /repo

✓ extension-memory extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-private-store.test.ts (3 tests) 66ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       3 passed (3)
Duration    1.06s
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The regression verifies that writing a dreaming private JSON artifact leaves the workspace root and `memory/` at `0770`, makes `memory/.dreams` private `0700`, writes the JSON file as `0600`, rejects paths outside `memory/.dreams`, and rejects a symlink-swap attempt before any outside artifact is written.
- **What was not tested:** Full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` was not run because this PR is intentionally narrow and the requested proof was Docker-targeted validation. Lint and broad check lanes were intentionally skipped.

## Additional Local Validation

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-private-store.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       3 passed (3)
```

```text
git diff --check
# no whitespace errors
```

## Not Run

- Full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` was not run.
- Lint/check lanes with high memory cost were intentionally skipped.
- `codex review --base origin/main` was not run locally for this replacement pass.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted change. I reviewed the previous ClawSweeper security-boundary finding, reworked the patch to keep workspace-root containment in the actual read/write path, and ran the Docker-targeted regression above.
target.author: llagoon3
